baseline_guidance: Run each eval with this skill and compare it against a no-skill baseline or previous-version snapshot using the same prompt and inputs.

evals.prompt: I have a rough product idea and a repo, but I don't know whether to grill it, write a PRD, make issues, or just implement. What Matt Pocock flow should I use?
evals.expected_output: The agent routes the user through the idea-to-ship flow, starting with grilling in the existing codebase and only moving to PRD/issues when the work is multi-session.
assertions: Names the main idea-to-ship route in order.
Starts with the codebase-aware grilling path for an idea in a repo.
Distinguishes same-session implementation from multi-session PRD and issue creation.
Mentions context hygiene or fresh sessions for implementation issues.

evals.prompt: I have a pile of incoming GitHub bugs and one separate design idea that has no repo yet. Which skill should handle each?
evals.expected_output: The agent separates raw incoming tracker work from stateless design sharpening, recommending triage for the bugs and grill-me for the no-repo idea.
assertions: Routes raw bugs or requests to triage.
Routes the no-codebase design idea to grill-me.
Does not send issues created by to-issues back through triage.
Explains why the two starting points differ.
